source: Purdue University Extension GDD reference tables, Cornell Cooperative Extension, UC Davis Vegetable Research and Information Center, USDA-ARS publications, OSU Extension, Washington State University Extension. Heat unit ranges represent the spread between earliest short-season and full-season cultivars in the literature; gardeners should choose a value within the range based on the variety they are growing.

entries.notes: Determinates cluster near 1200 to 1400 GDD. Indeterminates need 1500 to 1800. Heirloom beefsteaks sit at the top of the range.
Bell peppers ripen green at 1100 GDD, red ripe at 1500 to 1600. Heat units accumulate slowly in cool maritime summers.
Jalapeno 1400 to 1600, habanero and ghost types push 1800 to 2000. Heat-loving crop, base 60 is well established.
Long Italian types around 1500, large globe types up to 2200. Eggplant is the most heat-demanding common nightshade.
New potatoes at 1200 to 1400, full storage maturity 1500 to 1700. Tuber initiation begins around 600 GDD.
Husks fill at 1300 GDD, full ripe color shift at 1500 to 1700.
Slicing types 800 to 950, pickling 850 to 1100. Fruit set is rapid once vines flower.
First fruit at 700 GDD, peak production 850 to 1000. Plants out-pace harvest in warm weeks.
Pattypan, yellow crookneck, and straightneck all cluster at 800 to 1000 GDD to first harvest.
Delicata and acorn finish near 1500, full Hubbard and Cushaw types push 2000 to 2200.
Waltham butternut needs 1700 to 1900 for storage maturity; full hard shell at 2100 to 2300.
Table Queen and Honey Bear types finish at 1500 to 1700; mature winter storage at 1800 to 2000.
Pie pumpkins 1700 to 1900. Jack-o-lantern and giant types need 2000 to 2400.
Sugar Baby and other short-season types 1700 to 1900. Charleston Gray and full Crimson Sweet need 2200 to 2300.
Western shippers 1400 to 1600. Full eastern muskmelon types 1700 to 1900.
Honeydew is later than cantaloupe; expect 1700 to 2000 in most regions, 2200 in cool summers.
Early types head at 1200 to 1400 GDD from transplant. Calabrese and full season types 1500 to 1800.
Snowball and similar types around 1400 to 1600. Romanesco and storage types need 1700 to 1900.
Early flat types 1300 to 1500. Late storage cabbage and savoy types 1700 to 1900.
Brussels sprouts are the longest-season brassica. Plan for first frost to sweeten sprouts at the top of the range.
First leaf harvest at 800 GDD. Full mature plants and seasonal continuous picking through 1200.
Georgia and Vates collards reach full harvest size at 1000 to 1400 GDD.
Early types ready at 700 to 900. Storage kohlrabi (Gigante, Superschmelz) needs 1000 to 1100.
Baby bok choy at 600, full heads at 800 to 900. Quick spring or fall crop.
Mini types head at 900 to 1100. Full barrel-shaped napa needs 1200 to 1400.
Salad mustard at 600 to 700 GDD. Larger braising mustards 800 to 900.
Salad turnips (Hakurei, Tokyo Cross) at 800. Full purple-top storage roots 1000 to 1200.
American Purple Top rutabagas need 1300 to 1500. Fall storage size at 1500 to 1700.
Long-day storage onions 1700 to 2300 from set or transplant. Short-day southern types finish earlier in their narrow window.
Summer leeks (King Richard) at 1700. Winter-hardy leeks (Bandit, Lancia) need 2100 to 2300.
Spring growth GDD only; garlic is fall-planted, vernalized over winter, and harvested when 4 to 6 lower leaves brown. Treat range as spring season GDD from regrowth.
Shallots from sets, similar to onions but faster. French gray shallots toward the top of the range.
Bunching scallions ready at 700 to 1000 GDD; treat as cut-and-come-again.
Provider, Contender, and similar bush snap beans 700 to 900. Filet types 800 to 1000.
Kentucky Wonder, Blue Lake pole 1000 to 1200. Asparagus yardlong types push 1300 to 1400.
Bush limas (Henderson, Fordhook) 1300 to 1600. Pole limas (King of the Garden) 1700 to 1900.
Scarlet Runner and similar 1000 to 1400. Pollinator-favorite vine bean.
Vegetable soybeans 1500 to 1700 for fresh pod. Dry-down for storage at 1900 to 2000.
Sugar Ann (dwarf) at 800. Sugar Snap (tall) at 1000 to 1100.
Oregon Sugar Pod and Mammoth Melting Sugar 800 to 1100 GDD.
Lincoln, Wando, Maxigolt shelling peas 900 to 1200 from spring sow.
Windsor and Broad Windsor types 1400 to 1700. Cool-season legume; bolts in summer heat.
Kabuli (large white) chickpeas 1700 to 2000. Desi (small dark) 2000 to 2300.
Baby cut at 600. Full leaf rosette 800 to 900. Bolts above 1000 GDD when temps exceed 75°F.
Butterhead types 900 to 1100. Romaine and crisphead need 1100 to 1300.
Baby spinach at 500. Full bunching size 700 to 800. Bolts past 1000 GDD in long days.
Baby chard at 800. Full rainbow chard 900 to 1100. Continuous cut-and-come-again after first harvest.
Quickest of the leafy greens. Salad mix at 400 GDD, mature spicy leaves at 600 to 700.
Nantes and round types 1000 to 1200. Imperator and large storage 1300 to 1400.
Baby beets at 900. Mature storage roots (Detroit Dark Red, Chioggia) 1100 to 1300.
Round salad radishes at 350 to 450 GDD. Daikon and watermelon radish 500 to 550.
Long-season root. Hollow Crown and Harris Model 1500 to 1700. Sweetens after first frost; mature size by 2000 GDD.
Early-season sugary types (Trinity, Bodacious) 1400 to 1600. Mid-season SE types 1700 to 2000. Full-season Silver Queen, Country Gentleman 2200 to 2400.
Strawberry popcorn 1900 to 2100. Full Robust Yellow 2300 to 2400 to dry-down.
Field dent corn for grain. Reid Yellow Dent and Hickory King 2400 to 2600. Full southern dent types 2700 to 2800.
Short-season grain sorghum 1900 to 2200. Full-season tall varieties (Tarahumara, Black Amber) 2300 to 2500.
Proso millet (Panicum miliaceum) is the fastest grain. White Wonder and similar 1100 to 1300. Full grain harvest 1400 to 1500.
Grain amaranth (Burgundy, Golden Giant) 1300 to 1700. Leaf amaranth ready much earlier (300 to 500).
Spring-season GDD from regrowth to first ripe fruit. June-bearing types fruit at 600 to 800. Day-neutral and everbearing continuous from 800 to 1000.
First pods at 1500 GDD. Heavy production 1700 to 2200. The most heat-loving common annual; struggles below base 60°F.
Aunt Molly's and Cossack Pineapple types ripen at 1400 to 1600. Heavy drop and self-seed by 1800 GDD.
First cut at 600 GDD. Mature plants 800 to 900. Bolts past 1100 in heat; pinch flowers to extend.
Leaf cilantro at 450. Bolts quickly above 600 to 650 GDD; succession-sow for continuous supply.
Leaf dill at 600. Seed heads (for pickling) at 800 to 900. Bouquet and Mammoth Long Island are the standard types.
Italian flat-leaf parsley. First cutting at 900 GDD; biennial so spring-planted plants harvested through fall and overwintered in mild climates.
Indeterminate hybrid bred for early ripening; first ripe fruit at 1100 GDD, peak production 1300. Notably faster than beefsteaks.
Large fruited indeterminate types (Beefsteak, Better Boy, Big Beef) need 1500 to 1800 GDD. Heat units accumulate slowly in cool maritime summers.
Pink potato-leaf heirloom; 80 to 90 days from transplant. Indeterminate; full beefsteak fruit weight at 1700 GDD.
Italian paste indeterminate; first ripe at 1300, full canning maturity 1500. Heat tolerant; performs well in Mediterranean summers.
Cold-tolerant Czech indeterminate; sets fruit in cool soil. First ripe by 1000 GDD, the second-fastest tomato after Sub Arctic Plenty.
Russian heirloom that sets fruit at 38 to 40 F nights; first ripe 1000 GDD. Determinate; productive in zone 2 to 4 short seasons.
Beaverlodge AB Research Station release; the earliest open-pollinated tomato. First ripe at 900 GDD, the lowest in the species.
Tennessee dusky-purple heirloom indeterminate; first ripe at 1500 GDD, full flavor 1700 to 1750. Cracks if watering is uneven.
Determinate paste tomato; entire crop ripens in concentrated window between 1300 and 1500 GDD.
Tokita Seed indeterminate cherry hybrid; first ripe by 1100 GDD, continuous through frost. The early cherry standard.
Green jalapeno at 1300 GDD, red ripe at 1500 to 1600. Heat units accumulate slowly until soil hits 65 F.
Capsicum chinense; first ripe orange fruit at 1800 GDD, full red 2000 to 2100. Slow start, heavy late summer harvest.
New Mexico green chile; green fruit at 1400, red ripe 1600 to 1700. The NM market standard.
Bird's-eye chili; first green fruit at 1500, red ripe at 1700 to 1800. Highly productive once it starts.
Capsicum pubescens; tolerates cooler nights so base temp 55 (not 60). Long-season Andean species; full ripe 2000 to 2200.
Standard cayenne types; first red fruit at 1500, dry-ready (full red, dehydrated on plant) at 1800 to 1900 GDD.
Peruvian baccatum; long-season species. First orange-yellow fruit at 1800, full ripe and flavored at 2100 to 2300.
Yellow wax-type ripening to red. Yellow harvest at 1200, full red at 1500. Productive in short seasons.
Thin-walled frying pepper; first yellow fruit at 1100, full red 1300 to 1400. Fastest of the sweet types.
Cucurbita pepo; small thin-skinned squash, harvest at 1400 GDD for fresh eating, 1700 for storage maturity.
Japanese Cucurbita maxima; dense flesh requires 1700 GDD minimum, 2000 for full storage maturity.
Cucurbita maxima large keeper; 2000 GDD for harvest, 2400 for cured storage shell. Long-season crop.
Cucurbita pepo with stringy flesh; harvest when shell yellows at 1500, peak flavor 1700 to 1800.
Short-season icebox watermelon; 8 to 10 lb round fruits, ready at 1700 GDD.
Long oblong Charleston-type; full-season cultivar needing 2100 to 2300 GDD.
Round mid-season watermelon; 20 to 25 lb fruits, mid-range GDD at 1900 to 2200.
French petit melon; 2 lb fruits, intense aroma. Slip-ripe at 1400, sugars peak 1500 to 1600.
Mid-season eastern muskmelon; 4 to 5 lb fruits, ripe at 1500 to 1800 GDD.
Russian heirloom honeydew-type; pale yellow rind, ripe at 1600 to 1800 GDD.
Bush snap bean; first pick at 700 GDD, full production through 900.
Classic pole bean; first pick at 1100 GDD; continuous through 1300 GDD.
Phaseolus acutifolius; Sonoran-desert heat-adapted dry bean. Drier-tolerant base temp 55. Full dry-down 1100 to 1500.
Standard dry bean; full pod fill at 1500, dry-down for storage 1700 to 1800.
Caribbean dry-bean type; full dry-down at 1600 to 1900 GDD.
Large-seeded bush lima; first pod fill at 1400, full mature dry seed 1600 to 1700. Higher base temp than other beans.
Cool-tolerant Phaseolus coccineus pollinator vine; pods at 1000 GDD, dry beans 1200 to 1300.
Butterhead; small head at 750 GDD, full head 900 to 950. Bolts above 1100 in long days.
Standard cos types (Parris Island, Jericho, Coastal Star); first cutting at 1000, full head 1200 to 1300.
Mini-romaine; single-serving head at 800 GDD; full 1000. Slow-bolt for spring carryover.
Savoyed long-standing spinach; baby cut at 500, full bunch 700 to 750. Bolts above 1000 in long days.
Cut-and-come-again kale; baby leaves at 800 GDD, full plant size 1000 to 1100 GDD; continues through season.
French cylindrical Nantes type; harvest-size roots at 1000 GDD; full sweetness after first cool spell 1100 to 1200.
Long thin supermarket-style; needs longer to size up than Nantes. Full harvest at 1200 to 1400.
Common deep red beets (Detroit Dark Red, Chioggia, Bulls Blood); baby beets at 1000, full storage size 1200 to 1300.
Standard round salad radishes (Cherry Belle, French Breakfast, Easter Egg); full size at 350 to 500 GDD. Pithy past 600.
Grain amaranth (Amaranthus hypochondriacus); seed-fill at 1500, full dry-down 1700 to 1800.
Andean Chenopodium quinoa; full seed-fill 1700, harvest-ready dry seed 1900 to 2000. Heat above 95 F sets seedhead temperatures sterile.
Fagopyrum esculentum; first flowers at 400, mature seed 700 to 900. The fastest grain crop.
Setaria italica; seed-fill at 1300, full grain dry 1400 to 1500.
Eragrostis tef; Ethiopian grain. Seed-fill at 1500, full dry-down 1700 to 1800.
Late-season SU sugary corn; harvest at 2200 to 2400 GDD. Notably slower than modern SE types.
SE sugary-enhanced mid-season hybrid; harvest at 1500 to 1700 GDD.
Ipomoea batatas tubers; small new potatoes at 1500, full storage 1700 to 1900. Cure 2 weeks at 80 F after harvest.
LSU 1987 release; the dominant US commercial sweet potato. Storage roots fill at 1700, cure-ready at 1900 GDD.
Japanese supersweet (sh2) hybrid series; harvest at 1500 to 1700 GDD. Holds field-ready window longer than older SU types.
